summary: Added docs.json published-page filter to prepare.py, swapped one unpublished pilot page, added parity dimension prompt, scored all 10 pages on both dimensions. All validators pass.

unpublished_pages_swapped.reason: Not present in docs.json navigation — confirmed missing from Mintlify build
unpublished_pages_swapped.rationale: Same topic (message flagging), same Eastern cohort, published in docs.json

clarity_after_swap.change_from_0067: Identical avg and distribution — swapped page (message-flagging) scored 2, same as removed page (flag-unflag-a-message). Baseline unchanged.

lowest_pages.rationale: iOS/Android have 5 blocks each; TypeScript has 1; Flutter has 0 — >5x disparity.
Flutter has 1 block vs 3 for iOS/Android/TypeScript — 3x disparity, upload-progress and download missing.

parity_first_run.consistency_assessment: Parity scoring looks fair and signal-rich on multi-platform pages (pages with CodeGroups scored consistently by block-count analysis). Single-platform pages scored with 'low' confidence where applicable. The main v1 limitation is real: authentication.mdx scored 3 due to TypeScript having more depth, but this might reflect intentional platform differences — the low confidence flag on these cases is working correctly. v2 multi-file would resolve ambiguity. Overall: scoring appears consistent, not noisy.

changes.description: Added is_published() helper (recursive docs.json walker), load_published_pages(), --no-docs-json-filter flag. Filter active by default. Logs skipped_unpublished count. Fixed utcnow() deprecation.
Replaced flag-unflag-a-message.mdx (unpublished) with message-flagging.mdx (published, same topic, same cohort).
New: Cross-platform parity prompt. Rubric levels quoted verbatim. v1 single-page scope documented. Confidence=low guidance for ambiguous cases.
Regenerated with message-flagging.mdx replacing flag-unflag-a-message.mdx. Avg unchanged at 3.2.
New: first parity scores. avg=3.2, dist 2:2/3:4/4:4/5:0.
New: clarity score for replacement page (score=2, high confidence).

validators_run: python3 -c '...assert dimension==parity; assert pages_scored>=8; assert all scores 1-5; print OK clarity_pages=10 parity_pages=10'
